title: Nilavu Thoongum Neram Lyrics Kunguma Chimil
stanzas:
<div>
Nilavu Thoongum Neram Lyrics Kunguma Chimil

Nilavu thoogum neram
ninaivu thoongidaathu.
iravu thoonginaalum
uravu thoongidaathu

idhu oru thodarkathai
thinam thinam valarpirai
nilavu thoongum neram

naangu kannil indru
oru kaatchi aanathe.
vaanam kaatru boomi
ivai saatchi aanathe.

naan unai paarthadhu
poorva jenma bandham.
neenda naal ninaivile
vaazhum indha sondham

naan ini nee
nee ini naan
vazhvom vaa kanne.

Nilavu thoogum neram
ninaivu thoongidaathu.
iravu thoonginaalum
uravu thoongidaathu

idhu oru thodarkathai
thinam thinam valarpirai
nilavu thoongum neram

geethai pola kadhal
miga punithamaanathu.
kodhai nenjil aadum
indha siluvai pondrathu.

vazhvilum thalvilum
vilagidaatha nesam
vaalibam thedralaai
endrum ingu veesum

yaen mayakkam
yaen thayakkam
kannaa vaa ingae.

Nilavu thoogum neram
ninaivu thoongidaathu.
iravu thoonginaalum
uravu thoongidaathu

idhu oru thodarkathai
thinam thinam valarpirai

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song conveys themes of love and nostalgia, reflecting on memories that linger even as time passes.

In which movie is this song featured?

This song is featured in the movie Kunguma Chimil.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song is sung by the legendary S. Janaki and S. P. Balasubrahmanyam.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for this song was composed by the iconic Ilaiyaraaja.

When was this song released?

This song was released on August 23, 1985.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Nilavu Thoongum Neram, the lyrical beauty unfolds as the singers evoke deep emotions of love and reminiscence. The song serves as a poignant reminder of cherished moments, beautifully encapsulated in its soothing melody. As part of the movie Kunguma Chimil, it enhances the narrative by reflecting the characters' emotional journeys. The combination of heartfelt lyrics and masterful composition creates a timeless piece that resonates with listeners.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Nilavu Thoongum Neram from the timeless classic Kunguma Chimil is a mesmerizing melody that captures the essence of love and longing. With the enchanting voices of S. Janaki and S. P. Balasubrahmanyam, this song transports listeners to a world of nostalgia. The masterful composition by Ilaiyaraaja adds a unique charm, making it a must-listen for fans of old Tamil music.
stanzaMeanings: The time when the moon sleeps, memories do not fade. Even if the night sleeps, relationships do not fade.
This is a continuation, day by day, the growing moon sleeps.
Today, in my four eyes, there is a vision. The sky, the wind, and the earth are all witnesses.
Seeing you is a bond from a past life. This relationship lives on in memories for a long time.
Now, you and I, let’s live together, my dear.
The time when the moon sleeps, memories do not fade. Even if the night sleeps, relationships do not fade.
This is a continuation, day by day, the growing moon sleeps.
Like a song, love is very pure. This figure dances in the heart.
In life and in struggles, love does not fade, it always blows here.
Why this confusion, why this hesitation? Come here, my dear.
The time when the moon sleeps, memories do not fade. Even if the night sleeps, relationships do not fade.
This is a continuation, day by day, the growing moon.
The time when the moon sleeps, memories do not fade.
